preview_text:
The model makes Senegal the clear favorite at 61%, while Iraq carries a 12% win probability and occupies underdog status. Senegal's baseline strength supports the margin, though the model assigns Iraq meaningful upside relative to a typical +3 underdog in tournament play.

The 27% draw probability the model generates indicates a reasonably competitive match with genuine chances for a stalemate result. The spread between Senegal win (61%) and Iraq win (12%) is substantial but not extreme; this is neither a mismatch nor a toss-up. A draw ranks as the second-most-likely single outcome, suggesting tactical balance or defensive solidity may shape play.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket both shift sharply toward Senegal, pricing the win probability at 73–75% and compressing the draw to 17–18%. The model is 12 percentage points more bullish on a draw and correspondingly less confident in Senegal's outright victory. This divergence is material. The dashboard does not adjudicate whether the market is overweighting Senegal's favorite status or whether the model is overestimating draw likelihood given tournament conditions.
